things.name: Fes el-Bali Medina
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage world's largest living medieval city - 9,400 alleyways within the walls

things.name: Chouara Tannery
things.description: Ancient leather dyeing pits - view the rainbow of vats from surrounding leather shop balconies

things.name: University of Al-Qarawiyyin
things.description: Oldest continually operating university in the world, founded 859 AD

things.name: Bou Inania Madrasa
things.description: 14th-century Quranic school with the finest Marinid plasterwork and tilework in Morocco

things.name: Medersa Attarine
things.description: 14th-century madrasa adjacent to the Karaouyine mosque with exquisite stucco decoration

things.name: Borj Nord Fortification
things.description: 16th-century fortress with panoramic views over the ancient medina

things.name: Fes el-Jdid (New Fez)
things.description: Merenid royal city and the largest Jewish mellah in Morocco

things.name: Nejjarine Fountain & Fondouq
things.description: Restored 18th-century caravanserai with a wood arts museum

things.name: Bab Bou Jeloud (Blue Gate)
things.description: Ornate 1913 gateway into the medina - the iconic entrance to Fes el-Bali

things.name: Pastilla at Dar Roumana
things.description: Fez's signature pigeon or chicken pie in sweet-savoury pastry dusted with sugar
